This constraint prevents multiple entries for the same principal_id and capability, which is causing issues when trying to insert or update grants for different tenants with the same principal_id and capability.

contract_coverage.postcondition: The upsert operation should correctly handle grants for different tenants with the same principal_id and capability without violating the UNIQUE constraint.
The expiration boundary check should correctly identify expired grants without being affected by the UNIQUE constraint changes.

files.description: Remove the UNIQUE constraint on (principal_id, capability) to allow multiple entries for the same principal_id and capability across different tenants.
Ensure that the upsert operation correctly handles the new schema by updating or inserting grants without violating the UNIQUE constraint.

plan.notes: Removing the UNIQUE constraint on (principal_id, capability) allows multiple entries for the same principal_id and capability across different tenants. This change is necessary to resolve the data drift issue observed in the tests. The upsert operation must be adjusted to handle this new schema correctly. The expiration boundary check should remain unaffected by these changes.
